description: A national first-class museum showcasing artifacts from the Mawangdui Han Tombs and a collection of Hunan's historical and cultural heritage.
A famous mountain in Changsha with profound historical and cultural significance, featuring attractions such as Yuelu Academy.
The largest comprehensive park in Changsha and an important site commemorating revolutionary martyrs.
An ancient-style building constructed in honor of Du Fu, offering views of the Xiang River's night scenery.
A specialized museum displaying Hunan's geological resources and mineral specimens.
One of China's Four Famous Pavilions and the setting for Du Mu's poem 'Stopping by the Maple Woods in the Evening.'
The former residence of Jia Yi, a renowned Western Han statesman, a well-preserved ancient scholar's home.
A thousand-year-old academy, one of China's Four Great Academies, and the birthplace of Huxiang culture.
The largest public photography art gallery in China, regularly hosting photography exhibitions.
A historical and cultural street in Changsha that preserves traditional architectural styles and offers a variety of Changsha's local snacks.
A famous time-honored restaurant in Changsha, representing Xiang cuisine and the origin of snacks like stinky tofu.
A natural islet in the Xiang River, home to the Youth Mao Zedong Statue and a landmark attraction in Changsha.
A nostalgic-themed internet-famous restaurant known for its old Changsha street-style decor and signature spicy crayfish.
A thousand-year-old academy and a renowned higher education institution in China with a beautiful campus environment.
An artificial island in the heart of the lake, a model of modern urban planning, perfect for lakeside strolls with scenic views.
